result.summary: Mailgun exposes four authenticated developer surfaces—a Mailgun Email HTTP API, Validate HTTP API, Inspect HTTP API, and a local Mailgun MCP server—all using a Mailgun private API key from the dashboard, sent via HTTP Basic Auth (`api` as username) for the HTTP APIs and via `MAILGUN_API_KEY` for the MCP server.

mailgun_private_api_key.label: Mailgun private API key
mailgun_private_api_key.setup: Sign up or log in in the [Mailgun dashboard](https://app.mailgun.com/), then open [API Security](https://app.mailgun.com/settings/api_security) / **API Keys** and copy or create a **Private API key**. The docs say guided setup in the [welcome guide](https://app.mailgun.com/welcome-guide) creates your first API key, and the quickstart points to [API Keys](https://app.mailgun.com/settings/api_security). Use it with HTTP Basic Auth as username `api` and password = your private API key.

variables.name: region
variables.resolveFrom: Use EU server `https://api.eu.mailgun.net` instead of US when your account/domain is in EU, per docs and OpenAPI servers.
variables.description: Select US or EU API host.
